title: podman rootless v3.1 rc1 connection reset by peer
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
I have been using podman version 2.2.1 having downgraded due to version 3.0.1 giving me  (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer error.   Yesterday I tried upgrading to podman 3.1 rc1 and got the same error recv failure: connection reset by peer.
So, this bug is the same as #9532 but *not* a duplicate as it concerns a different version of podman.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  install podman version 3.1.0-rc1

2.  start a pod that contains containers, one of which is running a server.

3.  curl or use a browser to visit localhost at port 8000 for django.

**Describe the results you received:**
I get (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer

**Describe the results you expected:**
To have the html page returned

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I commented on #9532, and @sayap stated that version 3.1 rc1 works for him, on a gentoo install.  I am running on Silverblue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.1.0-rc1

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes

comments.body: Please provide the exact commands you use.

ok..
```
podman pod create --name pod_test -p 8000:8000
podman run -dit --pod pod_test --name test_cont
podman exec -it test_cont bash
```
inside of test_cont
```
pip install django
django-admin startproject test .
./manage.py runserver
```
inside test_cont, run curl 127.0.0.1:8000 and django install page is displayed.
in host run curl 127.0.0.1:8000 and connection reset error is displayed.
I have a poduction ready server running with a site deployed using podman 2.2.1 on the server, so I am keen to fix this.  If there is anything you need let me know.

This seems to be specific to django. Using the nginx image works fine.
I used the following steps to reproduce:
```
$ cat > Containerfile << EOF
FROM python

RUN pip install django && \
    django-admin startproject test .

CMD ./manage.py runserver
EOF
$ podman build -t django .
STEP 1: FROM python
STEP 2: RUN pip install django &&     django-admin startproject test .
--> Using cache 4d6cc6ca2b488f133afaf3aa6402820dd61c05b48073ab32bac4d9fa02f497cc
--> 4d6cc6ca2b4
STEP 3: CMD ./manage.py runserver
--> Using cache c8cf1ec4d882e88c8ea9ec4cb0b70c8887d28f1a5a4245e4c29adf0f5421c329
STEP 4: COMMIT django
--> c8cf1ec4d88
c8cf1ec4d882e88c8ea9ec4cb0b70c8887d28f1a5a4245e4c29adf0f5421c329
$ podman run -p 8000:8000 -d django
329d859b5518269765ff3f237468c999f941831ee6bcaee1db6f7e87b479d08f
$ curl 127.0.0.1:8000
curl: (56) Recv failure: Die Verbindung wurde vom Kommunikationspartner zurückgesetzt
$ podman exec -l curl 127.0.0.1:8000
works...
```
@giuseppe @AkihiroSuda Any ideas?

The issues is that django by default only listens on 127.0.0.1. Prior to 3.0.0 the rootlesskit port forwarder would always set the source IP to 127.0.0.1, this allowed django to work. This behaviour was intentionally changed, see CVE-2021-20199.

To fix your problem you must configure django to also listen on the slirp4netns tap interface. This can be done with `./manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:800`.
